regime_fall.current_status: Iran faces a complex crisis with a ceasefire extension offering temporary diplomatic respite, yet underlying tensions remain critical due to continued US naval blockade, Iranian port seizures, military posturing, fractured leadership dynamics, and Iranian refusal to negotiate under pressure. The regime demonstrates both military recovery capabilities and diplomatic intransigence while confronting internal instability and economic pressures.
regime_fall.last_major_event: On April 21-23, 2026, President Trump extended the US-Iran ceasefire indefinitely while maintaining naval blockades, US troop deployments accelerated to the region, Iran seized cargo ships in the Strait of Hormuz, and Iranian leadership rejected further negotiations under current conditions, signaling entrenched positions despite temporary ceasefire extension.
